title: Surround (the Plugin That Should Be Built In)
subtitle: ds, cs, ys — operate on surroundings as a first-class concept.
summary: Tim Pope's vim-surround adds three operators: ys (add surrounding), ds (delete surrounding), cs (change surrounding). Once you have it, it feels like core Vim.

blocks.text: Tim Pope's [vim-surround](https://github.com/tpope/vim-surround) is the plugin most often cited as "this should ship with Vim." It adds three operators that treat *surroundings* (quotes, brackets, tags) as first-class targets.

rows: Change surrounding tag (prompts for new tag name)
Surround inner word with single quotes

blocks.title: Why It Belongs in Core
blocks.text:
Vim's grammar already understands the *content* inside surroundings — `i"`, `i(`, `it`, etc. select the inner content. Surround completes the grammar by treating the *delimiters themselves* as a first-class concept that can be deleted, changed, or added.

The operator names are mnemonic: **d**elete-**s**urrounding, **c**hange-**s**urrounding, **y**ank-**s**urrounding ("surround with"). The plugin makes surroundings as natural to manipulate as words or lines — which is why most Vim users add it day one.

Neovim has discussed adding similar functionality to core; for now, install vim-surround.
